hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: What Are Your Plans? -- Launches, Meetings and Goals
lesson.menu_desc: O que vem pela frente: o plano do trimestre, a agenda de agosto e a apresentação para o time Global -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- What Are Your Plans?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Aula 5 -- What Are Your Plans?

listenings.text: Hello Emmanuele. Marc Dubois, from Paris. I have the dates for August, and I want to tell you the plan. We are going to arrive in Brazil on August twenty-fourth. On the first day, we are going to visit two hospitals with your field team. On the second day, you are going to present the business plan for Brazil. Twenty minutes, no more. And in the evening, we are having dinner with the global immunology team. That dinner is confirmed, so please come. One more thing. I am not going to ask you for fifty slides. I am going to ask you three questions. What is the goal, what is the strategy, and what is the deadline. Think about it. Talk to you soon.

Good morning, everyone. Claire Bennett here, from the global immunology team. This is a short message about the calendar for the next quarter, so you can organize your agenda. In May, we are going to announce the product approval in dermatology. This is very good news for our patients. In June, we are having the quarterly review, and every country is going to present the numbers. Brazil, you are first again. In July, the reimbursement decision is going to arrive, and it is going to change the plan for the public market. And in August, the global team is traveling to Brazil. The dates are confirmed. So, what are your plans for the next quarter? Please send me your business plan before the end of April. Thank you.

extra_audio.text: In April, I am going to send the business plan to Paris. In May, we are going to announce the product approval in dermatology. In June, I am going to present the numbers at the quarterly review. In July, the reimbursement decision is going to arrive. On August twenty-fourth, we are having dinner with the global team in Brazil.

items.situation: "So, what are your plans for the next quarter?"
tips: We are going to focus on dermatology and asthma.
We are going to work on the business plan for the private market.

items.situation: "What is the goal for the product approval?"
tips: We are going to get the product approval in May.
The goal is the product approval in dermatology.

items.situation: "And what is your strategy in the public market?"
tips: We are going to work with the reimbursement teams.
Our strategy is to organize more meetings with the public hospitals.

items.situation: "When are we meeting your field team?"
tips: We are meeting your field team on August twenty-fourth.
You are meeting the field team on the first day. It is confirmed.

items.situation: "Are you going to present the numbers in June?"
tips: Yes, I am going to present the numbers at the quarterly review.
Yes. I am going to present, and I am going to send the business plan before.

items.situation: The Global VP asks if the deadline in July is safe. Answer, and use the expression from today.
tips: It depends on the market conditions.
We are going to try, but it depends on the market conditions.
